# Contributing to Maester

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Maester.

The Maester team strives to maintain code that is resilient, safe, easy to understand, and easy to maintain. We welcome contributions that support these goals.

## Start Here

For full and up-to-date contribution instructions, please use the official [Maester Contributing Guide](https://maester.dev/docs/contributing).

For PowerShell coding conventions and rationale, please also review the [PowerShell Practice and Style](https://github.com/PoshCode/PowerShellPracticeAndStyle) guidance.

## Recommended PowerShell Practices

When contributing scripts, tests, or module changes, follow these baseline practices:

- Use the One True Brace Style (OTBS).
- Use Pascal Case for object and variable names.
- Use full function and cmdlet names instead of aliases.
- Include comment-based help inside function blocks (not above them) with a synopsis, description, and examples.

## Community Contribution Guidelines

- Keep pull requests focused on a single type of change.
- Add or update unit tests when changing behavior.
- Update relevant documentation when adding or changing commands/features.
- Follow existing patterns in the repository before introducing new ones.
- Use clear commit messages and pull request descriptions that explain the why. ([Conventional commit messages](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) are preferred.)
- Be respectful and constructive in all project interactions.

## Issues and Pull Requests

- Open an issue to discuss larger features or behavior changes before implementing.
- Link related issues in your pull request.
- When adding new tests, review [this issue](https://github.com/maester365/maester/issues/697) to check which test numbers have been used and indicate which you will use for your tests.
- Ensure automated checks pass before requesting review.
- Be responsive to review feedback and iterate on your changes.

## Licensing

By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the repository license.
